title: Spurious MULTIDRIVEN warnings with -fno-inline on cell output port connections
body:
## Bug

With `-fno-inline`, Verilator emits spurious `%Warning-MULTIDRIVEN` on wires driven by multiple cell instances' output ports. Without inlining, `instAll()` creates separate `AstAssignW` continuous assignments for each cell's output port connection, making the parent wire appear multi-driven. With inlining, each instance gets separate `__DOT__`-prefixed variables and the conflict doesn't arise.

```
%Warning-MULTIDRIVEN: Signal has multiple combinational drivers
```

### Failing tests (5)

- `t_cover_toggle` (x3 scenarios)
- `t_interface_star`
- `t_typedef_consistency_0`

### Reproducer

```bash
cd test_regress
verilator --cc --coverage-toggle -fno-inline t/t_cover_toggle.v
# -> %Warning-MULTIDRIVEN on 'toggle_up'
# Without -fno-inline, no warning
```

### Related

- Found during the `-fno-inline` audit for #1539 (post-scope inliner)

### Version

Tested on master at `bb0e1c8c6` (`Verilator 5.045 devel rev v5.044-152-gbb0e1c8c6`).
code_blocks:
%Warning-MULTIDRIVEN: Signal has multiple combinational drivers
cd test_regress
verilator --cc --coverage-toggle -fno-inline t/t_cover_toggle.v
# -> %Warning-MULTIDRIVEN on 'toggle_up'
# Without -fno-inline, no warning
